column3: Presence of blood in the urine
Coughing up (expectoration) of blood or blood-streaked sputum from the larynx, trachea, bronchi, or lungs
Lymphadenopathy: the abnormal enlargement of lymph nodes

An exanthema consisting of widespread pink-to-red macules (flat spots of 2-10 mm in diameter) or papules (red bumps) that blanch with pressure. The macules and papules may cluster and merge to form sheets over several days
Small hemorrhage in the skin, mucous membrane or serosal surface, or a group of hemorrhagic diseases characterized by purpuric lesions
Body temperature elevated above the normal range
A disorder characterized by marked discomfort in the throat
Enlarged lymph nodes in the neck
Pain in the adjacent areas of the teeth
A disorder characterized by lack of ability to open the mouth fully due to a decrease in the range of motion of the muscles of mastication
A state of sudden and severe confusion
Edema due to obstruction of lymph vessels or disorders of the lymph nodes
A red eruption of the skin
Enlargement (swelling) of a lymph node
Pain in calf
Increased resistance, involuntary flexure of the knee or pain in the calf upon forced dorsiflexion
Pain in leg
Swelling due to an excessive accumulation of fluid at a specific anatomic site
A sustained temperature of 38C (100.4F) or greater beyond the first 24 hours following birth, or a temperature of 39C (102.2F) within the first 24 hours following birth
A kind of short stature in which different regions of the body are shortened to differing extents
Benign hypertrophy that projects outward from the surface of bone, often containing a cartilaginous component
A small palpable mass in the subcutaneous tissues
The presence of erythrocytes with excessive variation in size in the blood
An abnormally large red blood cell occurring mainly in anemias (as pernicious anemia)
Anemia characterized by the presence of erythroblasts that are larger than normal
An increased concentration of glucose in the blood
